equipment_transitions.reason: Creates one corrective order and supersedes queued planned work.
The replacement decision is drawn once after crew arrival.
Completion may continue overdue maintenance with the same crew.
Only the unrepairable-failure branch is retained.
Replacement resets replacement and maintenance timestamps.
Completion reschedules the maintenance due event.
Arrival and operation start remain separately observable events.
A corrective work order selects repair or replacement without changing identity.
The direct branch is selected when maintenance is not overdue.
The overdue branch starts maintenance with the same crew.
Proactive age replacement is fixed off in Phase 1.
Planned and same-crew maintenance use one rule.

crew_transitions.reason: Dispatch is deduplicated once per timestamp.
Arrival selects and starts exactly one operation.
Completion exposes all same-time triggers before dispatch.
Returned crews become depot-idle before later dispatch.
Corrective priority and FIFO queues are explicit.
Away crews return only when both queues are empty.
Crew population is frozen at run start.
Mid-run workforce resizing is outside Phase 1.
Crew count is immutable in an experiment revision.

simultaneous_event_rule.reason: All corrective and planned request triggers at one time are visible before a free crew is assigned, preserving corrective priority.
